findings.title: Standalone `ingestcli` callers now panic unless metrics were initialized elsewhere
findings.why: This seems a bug: both new duration probes call `Observe` on package-level observers that stay nil until `ingestmetric.InitIngestMetrics()` runs. `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli` does not import `pkg/metrics`, so using this client from its own package tests or any standalone caller now adds a hidden global-init precondition and panics on the first `Recv`, `Close`, or `Ingest` path.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: Any code path that constructs `ingestcli` without going through the TiDB metrics bootstrap will crash instead of returning the HTTP result. The newly added `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli` unit tests already hit that path because they only import `ingestmetric`, not `pkg/metrics`.
findings.evidence: `WriteAPIDuration` and `IngestAPIDuration` are assigned only inside `InitIngestMetrics()` in `pkg/ingestor/ingestmetric/metric.go`, and that initializer is only invoked from `pkg/metrics.InitMetrics()`. The new `Observe(...)` calls in `startChunkedHTTPRequest` and `Ingest` run unconditionally, so a plain `newWriteClient(...).Recv()` or `client.Ingest(...)` on an uninitialized process dereferences a nil observer interface.
findings.change_request: Please initialize these observers in a way that does not depend on an external `pkg/metrics` side effect, or guard the `Observe` calls with a nil-safe fallback. Add a case for standalone `ingestcli` usage without metrics bootstrap so this contract stays enforced.
